description: The Supreme National Security Council (Shora-ye Ali-ye Amniat-e Melli, SNSC) was established by the 1989 constitutional revision to coordinate Iran's defence, intelligence, and foreign policy. The President chairs it; the Secretary — appointed with the Supreme Leader's approval — is effectively Iran's national security adviser and a direct representative of the [Office of the Supreme Leader](/orgs/office-of-the-supreme-leader). The SNSC has also been Iran's primary nuclear negotiating body. Key secretaries include [Hassan Rouhani](/people/hassan-rouhani) (1989–2005), [Ali Larijani](/people/ali-larijani) (2005–2007), and Saeed Jalili (2007–2013).
editorial_notes: Established 1989. President chairs; Secretary is de facto national security adviser. Nuclear file managed here under Rouhani, Larijani, and Jalili secretaryships.
